package router

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for router_setup_runs_migrations: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Gin router or handler setup code that also performs migrations.
type CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsModel
}

type CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsService struct {
    repo *CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveRouterSetupRunsMigrations(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsDTO) error {
    focus := "router_setup_runs_migrations"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    var request CaseRouterSetupRunsMigrationsDTO
    _ = c.ShouldBindJSON(&request)
    request.TenantID = c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
    c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": input, "tenant": request.TenantID})
    router_setup_runs_migrations_positive := map[string]int{"router": 1, "setup": 2, "runs": 3, "migrations": 4}
    router_setup_runs_migrations_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    router_positive_0 := c.FullPath() + ":" + input
    _ = router_positive_0
    setup_positive_1 := router_setup_runs_migrations_positive["setup"] + len(input)
    _ = setup_positive_1
    runs_positive_2 := router_setup_runs_migrations_positive["runs"] + len(input)
    _ = runs_positive_2
    migrations_positive_3 := router_setup_runs_migrations_positive["migrations"] + len(input)
    _ = migrations_positive_3
    return nil
}
